sanskrit:
न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे।
न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च॥
transliteration:
na ca śreyo ’nupaśyāmi hatvā svajanam āhave
na kāṅkṣe vijayaṃ kṛṣṇa na ca rājyaṃ sukhāni ca
literal_meaning: I do not foresee any good from killing my own people in battle, nor do I desire victory, O Kṛṣṇa, nor kingdom, nor pleasures.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna confronts the collapse of every external incentive—victory, sovereignty, enjoyment—when the cost is the death of those who constitute his inner world. In this moment he experiences a total erosion of meaning structures, revealing how identity anchored in roles and rewards disintegrates when faced with irreversible moral injury.
psychological_state: A state of acute existential depression where every future reward turns to ash because the psyche cannot integrate the cost of betrayal against one's own attachments.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving professional days before a hostile corporate takeover that will enrich them but bankrupt lifelong friends and mentors, feeling the promotion letter as a death warrant for their own capacity to feel joy.
anchor_text: When everything you fight for dies with victory, only duty remains.
